summary.intent: Fix two CI failures (Feature Flags — I18n cfg gate mismatch and a CRAP coverage regression) and address four P2 Codex code-review findings on the date picker component branch.
summary.outcome: All CI failures resolved, all four Codex findings implemented with tests, spec synced, and the branch merged with origin/main (which added the CRAP regression gate); 151 tests pass and clippy is clean.

repo: .crap-baseline.json is not committed to the repo — it is generated fresh each CI run by the CRAP gate (cargo xtask ci crap), so local baseline verification requires running coverage and cargo-crap manually.

ars-i18n coverage is measured via the wasm pipeline (web-intl feature), not the native-only lcov — cargo xci-fast's native-fast.lcov does not include ars-i18n, requiring a separate per-crate native lcov for CRAP checks on that crate.
The spec/components/date-time/date-picker.md snippet gate is syntax-only (syn::parse_file), so unqualified identifiers in snippets do not need to be in scope — no import preamble is injected.
The calendar component sets the parity reference for readonly semantics: allow opening, block selection mutations — date_picker had diverged by blocking opening entirely.
No date_picker adapter exists yet; the component is agnostic-only; Context docs referencing on_value_change adapter wiring were written ahead of the Props field existing.

code.finding: DateOrder is imported in the test module only under cfg(all(std, icu4x)), not plain cfg(icu4x) — tests that use DateOrder must match the all(std, icu4x) gate or use super:: qualification.
Context::requested_value doc references 'the adapter's on_value_change wiring' but Props::on_value_change did not exist — doc was written ahead of the field, confirmed as real drift.

Close-side mutations and effects (focus restore, open flag clear, requested_value clear) ran unconditionally on SelectDate even when the picker was already closed — must be gated on closes_open (open→close transition only).
parse_date and classify_input operated on raw input without normalizing localized (native) digits first — normalize_digits must be called before any ASCII digit parsing.

workflow: When a function's CRAP score spikes to 30.0 at 0% coverage after a behavior fix, the root cause is usually that the fix made an alternate path unreachable — add a direct unit test for the now-uncovered path rather than reverting the fix.

cargo-crap can be run against a per-crate native lcov (llvm-cov export --lcov) to verify CRAP scores locally when the full wasm pipeline is unavailable, filtering output to just the target crate avoids false 0%-coverage noise from unrelated crates.

When a spec and its implementation directly contradict each other (Props doc vs state machine comments + passing tests), check the sibling component for the canonical parity reference before deciding which side is wrong.

friction: clang-22 not available locally, making the full wasm coverage pipeline (needed for the merged native+wasm lcov the CRAP gate uses) unrunnable — had to verify CRAP scores via a separate per-crate native lcov as a proxy.
Branch was silently behind origin/main which had added a new CRAP regression gate; the CI failure gave no direct indication the gate itself was new, requiring manual git comparison to discover.

The CRAP regression was an indirect consequence of a correct bug fix (icu_date_order now succeeds for native-digit locales), making it non-obvious at fix time that fallback_date_order would lose coverage.
The spec had a direct internal contradiction on readonly semantics (Props doc vs state machine + deliberate comments + passing tests), requiring user escalation to resolve.
Session limit was hit mid-task, interrupting flow and requiring a cold-start continuation.
open_items: No date_picker adapter exists yet — Context docs reference on_value_change adapter wiring but only the agnostic component is implemented; adapter needs to be built separately.
Machine::transition has CRAP ~48 (cyclomatic-complexity-dominated) — flagged as unchanged but worth tracking; complexity went down slightly with readonly-guard removals but remains high.
wasm coverage pipeline not locally runnable (clang-22 missing) — CRAP verification for CI-merged lcov must be trusted to CI; local checks are native-only proxies.

New #4 readonly semantics reversal flipped three existing tests and changed deliberate behavior — the change should be flagged explicitly in the PR description so reviewers understand it is intentional.
